title: Marlierea karuaiensis
taxonomy: Marlierea karuaiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This family includes a wide variety of plants commonly known as myrtles or eucalyptus. Myrtaceae is a large and diverse family, comprising over 3,000 species found across the globe. These plants are often characterized by their showy flowers and aromatic leaves. They are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Marlierea karuaiensis is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits of antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Marlierea karuaiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
